'''Ben Jeffries''' (born 4 September 1980) is an Australian rugby league coach and former professional player who is the head coach of the [[Newcastle Knights Women]] [[NRL Women's Premiership]] team<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://postcourier.com.pg/jeffries-appointed-as-orchids-coach/ | title=Jeffries appointed as Orchids coach | date=9 February 2022 }}</ref>

A {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}}, he played the majority of his career for [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] after starting his career playing for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] and [[Wests Tigers]] in the NRL.

==Playing career==
===Early years===
Born in [[Forster, New South Wales|Forster]], Jeffries grew up in [[Forster, New South Wales|Forster]] and attended Forster High School, where he represented the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] in 1998 and was signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|accessdate=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=21 August 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080821104534/http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/player/103/Ben%20Jeffries|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120514230533/http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/player/103/Ben%20Jeffries|archive-date=14 May 2012|title=Bradford Bulls – Rugby League Championship}}</ref>

===St George Illawarra Dragons===
In 2000, after two seasons in the Knights' lower grades, Jeffries joined the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.jubileeavenue.com.au/history/history_players_jk.php|title=Dragons – Our Proud History – St George and Illawarra Rugby League|access-date=26 December 2018|archive-date=8 July 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190708032016/http://www.jubileeavenue.com.au/history/history_players_jk.php|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.dragons.com.au/lockeroom/profiles/players/ben_jeffries.htm |title=Ben Jeffries Player Profiles Locker Room St George Illawarra Dragons Rugby League |access-date=24 January 2001 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20010124083100/http://www.dragons.com.au/lockeroom/profiles/players/ben_jeffries.htm |archive-date=24 January 2001 |url-status=dead }}</ref> In Round 4 of the [[2001 NRL season]], he made his NRL debut in the Dragons 34–6 loss to the [[New Zealand Warriors]], his only appearance for the club.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2001/round-4/new-zealand-vs-st-george-illawarra/summary.html|title = NRL 2001 – Round 4 – Rugby League Project}}</ref>

===Wests Tigers===
In 2001, Jeffries joined the [[Wests Tigers]] halfway through the season, playing nine games. In 2002, he played 18 games for the side, starting 16 at halfback.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/about/wests-tigers-first-grade-players/ |title=Wests Tigers First Grade Players – Wests Tigers |access-date=26 December 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190531090443/https://www.weststigers.com.au/about/wests-tigers-first-grade-players/ |archive-date=31 May 2019 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTMtOC00LS0tLS0tMjM1MC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title = Custom Match List – Rugby League Project}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
In 2003, Jeffries joined [[Super League]] side [[Wakefield Trinity]], playing 27 games in his first season at the club.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2380677.stm|title=Wildcats sign up Aussie duo|date=31 October 2002}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/2395813/Sovatabua-puts-Castleford-out-of-action.html|title = Sovatabua puts Castleford out of action| date=9 February 2003 }}</ref> In 2004, he played 33 games, scoring 24 tries and leading Wakefield to the finals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/2395813/Sovatabua-puts-Castleford-out-of-action.html|title = Sovatabua puts Castleford out of action| date=9 February 2003 }}</ref> In 2005, he despite Wakefield's 10th-place finish, Jeffries scored 20 tries in 27 games, finishing as the club's top try scorer. Following his successful 2005 season, he signed a new three-year deal with the club.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/4709163.stm|title = Jeffries agrees new Wildcats deal|date = 22 July 2005}}</ref> In 2007, after 143 games for the club, Jeffries joined the [[Bradford Bulls]].

===Bradford Bulls===
In 2007, Jeffries played 27 games for the Bradford Bulls as they qualified for the finals series. On 11 September 2009, after two seasons with Bradford, he signed a three-year deal to return to Wakefield.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/news/4593440.Jeffries_back_at_Wakefield/ |title=Jeffries back at Wakefield | York Press |access-date=26 December 2018 |archive-date=26 December 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181226232707/https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/news/4593440.Jeffries_back_at_Wakefield/ |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/8250263.stm|title = Jeffries makes return to Wildcats|date = 11 September 2009}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity (second stint)===
In 2010, Jeffries played 22 games in his return season for Wakefield. On 7 May 2011, after playing five games for the Wakefield, Jeffries re-joined Bradford on an 18-month contract.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/13322517|title = Jeffries seals return to Bradford|publisher = BBC Sport}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (second stint)===
Jeffries appeared in every game for the rest of the 2011 season after returning to Bradford, starting at five-eighth from Round 14 to Round 27. In 2012, he featured in five consecutive games from Round 4 to Round 8. He missed Round 9 due to injury but returned a week later to play in nine consecutive games from Round 10 to Round 18. He also played in Round 20 and then from Round 23 to Round 26.

On 5 September 2012, he announced his departure from the Bradford Bulls to return to Australia to play part-time for the Kurri Kurri Bulldogs. At the time, Jeffries was the only Australian to play 10 consecutive years in the [[Super League]].

===Northern Pride===
In 2014 Jeffries signed to the [[Cairns]]-based [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] [[Queensland Cup]] side. He sustained an arm injury in his debut game and did not play again that season.

==Coaching career==
In 2014, Jeffries worked as an assistant coach for the [[Newcastle Knights]] [[SG Ball Cup]] side. Later that year, he joined the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] [[Queensland Cup]] side, playing one game, and working as the strength & conditioning and hookers & halves coach for their [[Cyril Connell Cup]] and [[Mal Meninga Cup]] sides.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.cairnspost.com.au/sport/pride-targetting-cowboys-forward-joel-riethmuller-for-2015-season/news-story/5f3544f8bad79103a1dd2e5b4e5b00a4|title = Riethmuller targeted|newspaper = The Cairns Post|date = 4 November 2014|last1 = O'Neill|first1 = Rhys}}</ref> In 2015, he left the club after playing three trial games, being granted a release.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.cairnspost.com.au/sport/pride-coach-ocallaghan-says-his-side-can-make-up-for-loss-of-ben-jeffries/news-story/2f24fe7c55a2b9b8c715b1faadd211e8|title=Northern Pride lose playmaker|newspaper=The Cairns Post|date=3 March 2015|last1=O'Neill|first1=Rhys}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2014/11/17/new-twist-for-nrl-journeyman/|title = New twist for NRL journeyman|date = 17 November 2014}}</ref>

In 2015, Jeffries began working for the NRL as a Development Manager in [[Townsville, Queensland|Townsville]]. In 2016, he coached the Townsville women's team and the NQ Marlins under-14 girls side. On 19 October 2016, he became head coach of the Queensland Murri women's team.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/10/19/queensland-murri-womens-squad/|title=Queensland Murri Women's squad|date=19 October 2016}}</ref>

In 2017, he joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] as an assistant coach their [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|under-20]] side under head coach [[Aaron Payne]]. Later that year, he was an assistant coach for the [[Queensland women's rugby league team|Queensland women's team]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/01/19/vowles-appointed-queensland-womens-coach/|title = Vowles appointed Queensland women's coach|date = 19 January 2017}}</ref>

In 2018, he was the assistant coach for the [[Townsville Blackhawks]] and head coach for the [[Queensland Academy of Sport|QAS]] women's under-18 squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2017/12/22/qrl-announce-2018-representative-staff/|title=QRL announce 2018 representative staff|date=22 December 2017}}</ref>

On 20 December 2018, Jeffries was announced as the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] Elite Pathways coach, taking over from [[Aaron Payne]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/12/20/cowboys-appoint-new-elite-pathways-coach/|title=Cowboys appoint new Elite Pathways Coach|date=20 December 2018}}</ref>

In 2020, Jeffries coached the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous Women's All Stars]]. He was again named head coach of the side for 2022 and 2023.<ref>{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald | title='Let our voices be heard': Women not consulted over anthem axing|first=Sarah|last=Keoghan| url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/let-our-voices-be-heard-women-not-consulted-over-anthem-axing-20200210-p53zi1.html}}</ref> In 2022, he coached the [[Papua New Guinea women's national rugby league team|PNG Orchids]] at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://postcourier.com.pg/jeffries-appointed-as-orchids-coach/ | title=Jeffries appointed as Orchids coach | date=9 February 2022 }}</ref>

On 24 November 2022, he was announced as the [[North Queensland Cowboys Women]] inaugural [[NRLW]] head coach.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2022/11/24/cowboys-announce-womens-leadership-structure/|title=Cowboys announce women's leadership structure|date=24 November 2022|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref> On 15 December 2023, he was released by the club.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2023/12/15/club-statement-ben-jeffries/|title=Club statement: Ben Jeffries|date=15 December 2023|website=NQ Cowboys}}</ref>
